q1.prompt: Which of the following buffer pairs is quantitatively the most important in the extracellular fluid (ECF) and has a pK of 6.1?

options.label: HCO_3^-/CO_2
Organic phosphates
Plasma proteins

q2.prompt: In which segment of the renal tubule does approximately 85% of filtered bicarbonate reabsorption occur under normal physiological conditions?

options.label: Thin descending limb
Proximal tubule
Thick ascending limb
Distal convoluted tubule
Cortical collecting duct

q3.prompt: Which enzyme is responsible for the reversible hydration of carbon dioxide to carbonic acid within both red blood cells and renal tubular cells?

options.label: Carbonic anhydrase
Glutaminase
Na^+/K^+ ATPase
Phospholipase C
Adenylate cyclase

q4.prompt: Which of the following describes the primary mechanism of H^+ secretion by the α-intercalated cells of the late distal tubule and collecting ducts?

options.label: Passive diffusion along an established electrochemical gradient
Na^+-H^+ exchange in the apical membrane
Facilitated diffusion via specific ligand-gated proton channels
Primary active transport via H^+ ATPase and H^+/K^+ ATPase
Secondary active transport coupled to the reabsorption of chloride

q5.prompt: How does a chronic increase in arterial P_(CO2) affect the renal handling of filtered bicarbonate?

options.label: Decreases reabsorption by inhibiting the luminal Na^+-H^+ exchanger
Decreases reabsorption by reducing the availability of intracellular hydrogen ions
No effect, as bicarbonate reabsorption is independent of carbon dioxide tension
Increases reabsorption by inhibiting the basolateral Na^+/K^+ ATPase
Increases reabsorption by increasing the availability of intracellular H^+ for secretion

q6.prompt: Which statement best describes the "diffusion trapping" of ammonia in the renal collecting duct during states of acidosis?

options.label: NH_4^+ diffuses from the renal cell into the lumen and is subsequently converted to NH3
NH_3 diffuses into the acidic lumen and becomes NH_4^+, which is not lipid-soluble and cannot cross back into the cell
NH_3 is actively pumped into the lumen by a specific primary active ATPase
NH_4^+ is filtered at the glomerulus and completely reabsorbed in the proximal tubule
Both NH_3 and NH_4^+ are freely permeable across all segments of the collecting duct

q7.prompt: A patient presents to the emergency department with severe, watery diarrhea and the following arterial blood gas results: pH 7.25, Pa_(CO2) 24 mm Hg, and [HCO3 -] 10 mEq/L. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

options.label: Metabolic acidosis with respiratory compensation
Acute respiratory acidosis
Chronic respiratory alkalosis
Metabolic alkalosis with respiratory compensation
Mixed respiratory and metabolic alkalosis

q8.prompt: A patient experiencing persistent vomiting of gastric contents is evaluated for acid-base status. Which of the following describes the initial renal correction mechanism for this state of metabolic alkalosis?

options.label: Increased secretion of ammonium (NH4+) to buffer additional acid
Increased reabsorption of filtered bicarbonate via the proximal tubule
Decreased urine pH to conserve hydrogen ions in the extracellular fluid
Increased excretion of bicarbonate due to the filtered load exceeding the renal reabsorptive capacity
Inhibition of the H^+/K^+ ATPase in the α-intercalated cells to prevent potassium loss

q9.prompt: A 19-year-old man is admitted after ingesting antifreeze. His blood tests reveal: Na^+ 135 mEq/L, Cl^- 100 mEq/L, and [HCO3 -] 5 mEq/L. Which of the following is his calculated anion gap, and what is the associated diagnosis?

options.label: 10 mEq/L; normal anion gap metabolic acidosis
25 mEq/L; increased anion gap metabolic acidosis
30 mEq/L; increased anion gap metabolic acidosis
35 mEq/L; normal anion gap metabolic acidosis
40 mEq/L; metabolic alkalosis with an osmolar gap

q10.prompt: A patient is found comatose and presents with an arterial blood sample yielding a pH of 7.12, a Pa_(CO2) of 60 mm Hg, and a plasma [HCO3 -] concentration of 19 mEq/L. Which of the following describes this patient's acid-base status?

options.label: Simple acute respiratory acidosis
Simple metabolic acidosis
Simple chronic respiratory alkalosis
Mixed metabolic alkalosis and respiratory acidosis
Mixed respiratory and metabolic acidosis
